title: Rich Silky Croatian Goose Confit
description: A slow-cooked, indulgent dish from Croatia that combines the richness of goose fat with the subtle umami of kombu extract.

ingredients.name: kombu seaweed
ingredients.notes: soaked in water for at least 4 hours

instructions: Rinse the soaked kombu seaweed and place it in a large saucepan.
Add the melted goose fat, minced garlic, and lemon juice to the saucepan.
Place the goose legs in the saucepan and bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
Transfer the saucepan to the preheated oven and cook for 3 hours.
Remove the saucepan from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the goose meat into tender strands using two forks.
Strain the kombu extract through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan, discarding the solids.
Whisk the strained kombu extract into the shredded goose meat.
Season the goose confit with black pepper to taste.
Serve the goose confit warm or at room temperature, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
